reviews.review_description: But the tracking needs work, if there was a setting to open up the hit box range on blocks it would be nice, ive VERY carefully followed instructions on the tutorial and still the blocking is only 50/50. If this was fixed I'd get rid of every other fitness game I ever bought.
Very fun and rewarding  VR title,the movement is awesome,alot of ducking and dodging and blocking and punching,just imagine the dummy is your boss,lol. Still one of the greatest VR games to date.

reviews.review_title: Not what i thought it was
reviews.review_description: I thought this game would teach you some kung fu but it teaches you nothing. Just puts u front of a pole with spinning things coming around the pole at different heights and speeds. No techniques or anything.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback, I understand what you expected.

Crazy Kung Fu isn’t designed as an instructional kung fu class with step-by-step techniques or a coach explaining forms. Instead, it’s built as a training experience. The dummy, timing, and scoring system guide you toward proper movement, structure, speed, and control through repetition and progression.

You improve by refining your form, accuracy, and flow. The leveling and scoring are designed to reward correct structure and efficient motion. It teaches fundamentals through doing, not through verbal instruction.

That said, I completely understand that if you were looking for a guided martial arts lesson, this might not match that expectation.

Appreciate you giving it a try.

reviews.review_description: Mid high blocks and mid low blocks are absent from the game, no big deal. As far as the score and timing to score I noticed inconsistencies, but as far as warming up and being beneficial to training I give it a 7 out of 10. It's a good starter for a beginner in martial arts. If you're are already good at blocking and striking it's a great warm up...just don't extend your elbows 😆. It's fun, can't wait for the update!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the review! More content to come this year, stay tuned.

reviews.review_title: It's alright but the martial arts is meh
reviews.review_description: I've seen other practioners complaining and well I can see why got to around level 9 and the detection for timing and range will just complete break even the detection in general will break punches read as blocks or misses even when hit perfectly punches expect you to punch when something has gotten way to close and you only have your arm about a quarter of the way out and the block timing just feels very inconsistent sometimes you have to block very far out (bad structure and too open in the real world) or you have to block super close for no reason take out the fixed distance and timing thing the fit more in line with real life and it wouldn't be a bad training game but in its current state it is very meh I was expecting more as well like the wing chun wooden dummys and all that fun stuff
This is a fun workout game to develop a  basic understanding of Kung Fu and martial arts footwork and reflexes. It would be amazing if there was a horse stance training feature

You only just punch or block and dodge in this game i punch a pad and it marks it at a block i block and it marks as a punch, and the annoying person at the back say terrible job when i do the right thing and the character is a picture the physics of the game is bad and this game is low quality not good for fifteen bucks.

Loving this game for workout and reflex training

Constructive criticisms

I would like more feedback on what is wrong with accuracy in training and workout mode... is quite frustrating when hits aren't registering and you don't know why

Some more details on how it is scored, some of the high scores seem very high, not sure how they get that high... other than punching very hard? :)

Thanks for a great game

reviews.review_title: The score requirements are way too high
reviews.review_description: If I don't lose all my hearts, I should be able to advance levels, I made it to expert section, level six I think, but it is just not fun replaying a level over and over with the annoying character in the background I can't turn off. The score is way too reliant on the multiplier. The detection for blocking is atrocious, it feels like it is a centimeter size zone to not get an error. The fov for workout is way too small, in the little previews the fov is much bigger. The fight mode is bad, it gets too close. All in all, if I hit the pad or block the stick, it should count a hit/block and the character in the back should NOT yell at me for "messing up", because I didn't. There is a reason the majority of games with score have perfect,great,okay,bad,and miss for hits(or some combination). This one is basically perfect or miss, not fun, frustrating.

I enjoyed this game while it worked. Shortly after I purchased it I bought a DLC pack mostly to support developers because I felt this app wasn’t getting the love it deserved. I never got the DLC content and now the app doesn’t work at all. It keeps telling me my system is updating and the update never happens. This isn’t the first problem I’ve had with stuff purchased from META and I can’t recommend anything they sell at this point. I contacted them months ago about the DLC content and problems not resolved and have been waiting days to have them respond to my report that the app isn’t working at all. I worry, how many other apps will do this? Really disappointed and fed up with META. 

*UPDATE:  Game works now but still never got DLC content I paid for. Great game , but I wouldn't buy any add ons for this one.


Here’s my original review:
This game was a pleasant surprise! Amazing workout for both your upper and lower body and FUN! I’ll be using this one a lot.

I give you a  good review I never received the bracers for g
ive you a 5 star now I changed 1 one star. Don't lie to your costumer. A soon I have the bracer you going to get the 5 star again.

reviews.review_title: As a Chinese Kung Fu practitioner…
reviews.review_description: Hi devs. I appreciate the effort in creating a Kung Fu game. It’s honestly not a bad attempt and I do enjoy the light workout it provides. However as a Chinese Kung Fu practitioner I have higher hopes in that the game produces more historical and cultural accuracy. Terms like Dojo which describe a Japanese martial arts school should be changed to Mo Kwoon or Wu Guan as that’s a Chinese term. Also, try to involve actual Wing Chun, Hung Gar, etc. teachers to show you the forms so you can put them into the game. That way people can actually learn all of the forms such as the fist forms and wooden dummy forms in real life, instead of just bashing a wooden pole at random. Wouldn’t that be a cool idea? If you need help just reach out and ask me as I study both Wing Chun and Hung Gar and I can help with the game.

Loved the first hour (except, no quit button??).  Next 15-30 minutes frustrating as there were seemingly huge jumps in difficulty at training 8 and fight 3, with insufficient in-game instructions on scoring to know how to proceed.  Last 15-30 minutes, huge bugs appeared:  training and fights wouldn't end or tally my score.  It's become virtually unusable.  Was there a bad update, or are there bugs, or did I get some kind of weird virus on my headset??  Return here at 1hr45mins??  Will wait a little in case the developer has a good response.

I took kung fu for years and was surprised and happy to see that this game, as much as possible really replicates the actual art. There are numerous clever ways of training including a moving opponent that’s my favorite part. 5 stars to the devs.

Boughtbumped my score up a point, bc this kind of game / experience is what vr is all aboit. It's different, engaging. 

it, got annoyed, refunded it, then decided to buy and give it another try, bc I do like the game concept. 

Issues: 

1) blocking bar ignores 1/3 of valid  blocks. Makes hard to move to new levels, since blocking is a major score contributor. And you need a certain score to unlock next level. 

2) Not a fan of forced perfectionism with the white silhouettes telling you when to block or strike. But, mod section on training has an infinite and no damage setting that lets you basically play "casual" mode to ignore that. 

3) Fight mode needs infinite mod to keep fighting as long as you'd like. 

4) Workout mode needs mods to go infinite instead of stopping every 2 mins. 

5) All modes would be nice to have mods to turn off certain arms, eg like no blades to just practice punch and block. 

6) RNG throws odd combos, like  rabbit punch pad and long blade to dodge. Or two block bars or punch pads on same side guaranteeing one fails. 

7) Rabbit punch pad is so small, it's easy to whack controllers into each other trying to hit. 

8) Blocking seems geared towards shorter/ narrower folks. I'm 6' broad shoulders, and shoulder rotator cuffs start taking a beating trying to pivot forearm up and down to block where white silhouette is so close to face and body. I offset this by leaning away from blocks to give shoulder less drastic angles of movement. 

9) Close blocks also sometimes cause controller to bash face visor. IRL, yes, you, want to block close. But, this is a game, and there's a bulky thing on my face. Need some allowance for that. 

10) Tutorial needs to clarify that "Mod" section is game modifiers, not for installing user mods. Other games have "mod" for user gen stuff. So, I ignored it first time not realizing that's where infinite mode and other goodies are. 

11) Fight mode Range score seems bugged. I'm up to 9th level on Training, and regularly get 90%+ on Range. But, I can't get past lv 3 Fight, bc Range is regularly capping at 50-60%. It tells me to close in on the dummy and duck blades. I'm already ducking top blades. I'm 6', so not going to duck mid or low blades, esp since I can't see what's above when coming back up unless I do some Neo sh*t in Matrix. I can't get any closer to the dummy, either. I'm as close as I can get without it moving away. So, I don't know why I get good Range in Training, but struggle in Fight. But, since I can't pass lv 3 Fight I can't unlock Events. 

Overall, glad I bought this again. You don't work up much of a, sweat in early Training levels that focus on getting technique down. But when you unlock later training levels, or go into Fights, you're gonna start getting your sweat on. That's why I want to unlock more Fight levels. They're a great workout.
Super great love the combat and the levels and everything is just so good

pro: simple wow. it's very funny.
con: No handtracking :(

reviews.review_title: Fantastic work out and lots of fun!
reviews.review_description: Keeps you moving. Up on your feet and moving constantly, there's no option to sit down and relax. It's a great workout.

This game is amazing, I have played for hour on end and I never get fed up with it, it’s like my warm up go to, but it’s that additive the battery’s dead before I know it, 5/5 all day!! Treat yourself it’s worth it!!
This is real fu-n! Good for getting your reaction time up, and can get suprisingly physically involving when you get immersed.
Training and training as a young KungFu. You will have more reflexes to dodge and will do nice exercises.
Best game on the oculus. A lot of replay value. Well worth $10. Buy it.

This game is amazing, it helps your reaction time and shows you what to do if that makes sense. Is it worth the $10? Yeah, I would say so. Makes you sweat also. Some people arent looking for that but some people are. If you're looking for a game that makes you sweat and helps your reaction time and is fun, then this game is probably for you.

you learn no real moves and how to get better other than moving fast and hope it's hood enough. the wing chun is cool but again there's no sequence u just guess on how you're suppose to hit yhr the targets.

developer_response.body: Hey, sorry to hear you're having a hard time understanding how to use the app. There is a fair amount of guidance for your Kung Fu journey: the tutorial, the ghost outlines for timing, the tips to explain each section, and then the feedback at the end of each level on your performance. Though I'd be more than happy to get some more details on exactly what you are struggling with, I may have missed something. You can email me: support@fieldof.vision. We also have a large Discord community with many experienced players who can help you out as well: https://discord.gg/hjupHpa

reviews.review_description: I played this game for hours on steam, and now it’s on quest! This game always leaves me tired and sweaty, and if you want to work your reactions, stamina, and focus: this is THE game to get. This game is one of the most high-energy games i’ve ever played!
I absolutely love this game.

I mainly use my quest for a workout, primarily playing Beat Saber and other rhythm based games, but when I need a break from that kind of excercise this game is great for getting my sweat on. 5 stars.
I love this game

I just got this like ten minutes ago and already addicted to it it is just so fun and a grate way to keep fit my vr was low so could only play for ten but already a grate game I recommend getting it when you have the chance

Do you enjoy realistic combat style games? 
Then BUY THIS NOW!  You won't regret it! 

Crazy Kung Fu is fun, a good workout, & an excellent addition to your fight training type activities!  Also, its price point is a real BARGAIN compared to other realistic fight-style games!  

I've studied martial arts, so I am excited to be able to practice & "fight" with a virtual Wing-Chun style dummy.  The dummies attacks are randomly generated, so there are no repetitive patterns to "learn".  Your timing & reflexes will get better with practice! 

Once you unlock "fight mode", the dummy becomes mobile, & its even more fun & challenging!  Make sure to have a 2meter square area to fight in tho! 

The developer is VERY engaged.  He has a FREE discord server where he interacts DIRECTLY with players giving tips & taking suggestions.  His updates reflect the community's input for improvements.  

A more detailed "how to punch/block" & body position demo for beginners would be helpful for those who are not yet familiar with martial arts.

This game lacks the ability to explain why I can't advance to the next level. I have continually worked hard on hitting every pad and blocking every stick, but I can't seem to advance to the next level. The point system is broken and doesn't allow many options for earning more points. Do I need to hit harder or softer? Do I need to block with aggression? Fix the point system, explain the colors and what they mean. Is there a strategy guide for advancing?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, hopefully I can help out a bit here. The main menu scroll has an explanation for each move when you select the tutorial button, this should clarify some parts.
As a very brief breakdown of the mechanics: the harder you punch, and the more still your arm is when you block, the higher the points. Maintaining a high combo multiplier for as long as possible is also one of the best ways to get a lot of points. If you want to discuss tips with others, join the Discord: https://discord.gg/hjupHpa There's even a tips channel with a lot in there already to help you get started.

reviews.review_description: This game is very physically challenging, and gives a great workout. It will make you sweat a lot, and train you hard. Well worth it to get and play, for a fun challenging fitness based game.

It's a great game that tests your reflexes and keeps you moving the whole time. The kung-fu theme to it just makes it very appealing and engaging. The graphics are nice and the game feels very responsive in all situations.

reviews.review_title: By far my favorite fitness app for VR
reviews.review_description: Hands down my favorite fitness app on Meta Quest 2. Thanks to the developers for making some thing that ￼is both fun and a really good workout!!

This game is awesome. I have a lot of fun trying to get a good report with my blocking and punching. but the old man keeps telling me to try try again LOL. Its all about timing with this one. but great game to get a little workout in. You don't realize you are actually working out. great to warm me up for a full on workout.
great exercise and lots of fun
Great game
Need to be a way to reset view so you can move your start position

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review. On Meta Quest you can reset your view by holding down the Menu button on your right controller. You can do this anytime during the game.
If you have further issues or comments feel free to reach out: support@fieldof.vision

reviews.review_description: Amazing game! Very enjoyable. Cant stress enough, you all should try this. Not just a game but a way to learn self defense and the best part you will have alot of fun learning. To be honest I got hooked easily and not only that when you see yourself on the leaderboards its a different felling man. You guys gotta try this for sure. Very good community and responsive dev.

reviews.review_title: Even with back problems, this app rocks
reviews.review_description: I have back problems so I was concerned about this app hurting my back. After three days with it I have had no problems. The app is fun, gives me a good workout, and is building a new skill set for me. This is a winner.
